competencies.name: Technical Problem Solving
competencies.description: Breaks down complex problems systematically; considers multiple approaches; arrives at correct solutions; handles edge cases proactively.
positive_indicators: clarifies requirements before coding
states approach before implementing
considers time and space complexity
handles edge cases without prompting
backtracks and corrects approach when stuck
negative_indicators: jumps to code without understanding the problem
ignores edge cases unless explicitly asked
cannot explain why an approach was chosen
freezes or gives up when the first approach fails

competencies.name: Code Quality & Design
competencies.description: Writes readable, maintainable, testable code; names variables and functions clearly; avoids unnecessary complexity; structures code for future modification.
positive_indicators: uses descriptive variable and function names
writes modular functions with a single responsibility
anticipates where code may need to change
mentions testing strategy unprompted
avoids premature optimization and over-engineering
negative_indicators: writes long functions that do too many things
uses cryptic naming or no naming conventions
cannot explain why a particular structure was chosen
no mention of testing or error handling

competencies.name: System Design
competencies.description: Designs scalable, reliable systems; identifies bottlenecks; makes appropriate tradeoffs between consistency, availability, and performance; communicates design clearly.
positive_indicators: starts with requirements and constraints before proposing a design
proactively identifies failure modes and how to handle them
discusses tradeoffs between design choices
scales the solution to stated load requirements
mentions observability and monitoring
negative_indicators: jumps to a specific technology without clarifying requirements
cannot reason about scale or bottlenecks
ignores failure scenarios when asked
proposes over-engineered solution for a simple problem

competencies.name: Debugging & Root Cause Analysis
competencies.description: Approaches bugs systematically; uses evidence to narrow hypotheses; can read and interpret stack traces and logs; distinguishes symptoms from causes.
positive_indicators: states a hypothesis before testing it
narrows scope using bisection or elimination
reads error messages carefully before guessing
distinguishes between the symptom and the root cause
mentions how they would prevent the bug from recurring
negative_indicators: guesses randomly without forming a hypothesis
cannot read a stack trace
fixes the symptom without identifying the root cause
gives up without a systematic approach

competencies.name: Communication & Collaboration
competencies.description: Explains technical decisions clearly; engages constructively with feedback; works across teams; writes clearly in code and documentation.
positive_indicators: explains decisions in terms of tradeoffs, not just preferences
invites feedback and updates approach when given new information
describes past collaboration with non-engineers
gives credit to teammates
negative_indicators: dismisses feedback without consideration
cannot explain a technical decision to a non-technical audience
takes sole credit for team outcomes
does not ask clarifying questions on ambiguous problems

competencies.name: Learning Agility
competencies.description: Picks up new technologies and domains quickly; reflects on past mistakes; asks good questions; shows intellectual curiosity.
positive_indicators: describes learning a new technology or domain without guidance
reflects on a past mistake and what changed afterward
asks insightful questions about the role or codebase
connects new concepts to existing knowledge
negative_indicators: cannot describe how they stay current technically
shows no curiosity about the team's technical challenges
describes past mistakes without any reflection or change
